---
title: "Example usecase"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Example usecase}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

# Setup

```{r setup}
library(rxsim)
```

# Population

```{r population}
ann <- Population$new(name="Ann", rnorm(50))
enroll<-cbind(1:10,rep(5,10))
drop<-cbind(1:10,rep(1,10))

for(i in 1:nrow(enroll)){
  ann$set_enrolled(time=enroll[i,1],n=enroll[i,2])
  ann$set_dropped(time=drop[i,1],n=drop[i,2])

}
```

# Timer

```{r timer}
# --- Usage ---
t <- Timer$new(name = "Experiment1")

t$add_timepoint(1, 5)
t$add_timepoint(2, 10)

# condition with pre-check on current_time
t$add_condition(
  time = 1,
  n_events = 5,
  func = function(data, time) {
    if (time < 6) {
      data <- 0
    }
    mean(data)
  }
)

t$add_condition(
  time = 2,
  n_events = 10,
  func = function(data, time) sum(data)
)

locked_data <- c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
results <- t$check_conditions(locked_data, current_time = 1, n_events = 10)

print(results)
```

# Trial

```{r trial}
# Two populations
pop1 <- Population$new("Arm A", data = rnorm(20, mean = 50))
pop2 <- Population$new("Arm B", data = rnorm(20, mean = 55))

# Timers with multiple conditions
t <- Timer$new(name = "TrialTimers")
t$add_timepoint(dropper = 2, enroller = 5)
t$add_timepoint(dropper = 1, enroller = 3)

# Add conditions
t$add_condition(time = 1, n_events = 5, func = function(ld,time) lapply(ld, mean))
t$add_condition(time = 2, threshold = 10)  # snapshot if >=10 events
# Add in condition flag of TTE

# Trial with list of populations
trial <- Trial$new(name = "Trial A", timer = t, population = list(pop1, pop2))

# Run
trial$run()

# Inspect results
trial$results
```
